title: hare-lsp settings
description: Canonical schema for the `hare.*` configuration namespace consumed by the hare-lsp language server via `workspace/configuration`. Editor integrations should keep their own settings UI in sync with this document.

path.description: Path to the `hare` binary used by the language server for type-check diagnostics and tooling subprocesses.

harepath.description: Colon-separated module search path (overrides $HAREPATH for the server). Empty falls back to $HAREPATH from the server's environment, then the platform default.

tags.description: Build tags passed to `hare build` and module resolution, e.g. ["+linux", "+x86_64"].

debounceMs.description: Minimum milliseconds between the last `didChange` and the next parse-diagnostics refresh.

enableBuild.description: Run `hare build` on save to surface type-check diagnostics in addition to the in-process parse pass.

buildTimeoutMs.description: Maximum wall-clock milliseconds to wait for `hare build`/`hare test`/`hare run` before SIGTERM. Set to 0 to disable the timeout.

format.description: Formatter output options. Applied by textDocument/formatting and friends.

indentWidth.description: Spaces per indent level when `indentStyle` is `space`. Ignored when `indentStyle` is `tab`.

trimFinalNewlines.description: Trim trailing whitespace at end of file.

insertFinalNewline.description: Ensure the file ends with a single newline.

inlayHints.description: Inlay-hint visibility toggles. The server still computes hints when these are false; the client typically hides them. Both default on.

parameterNames.description: Show parameter-name hints at call sites.

inferredTypesMaxDepth.description: Maximum recursion depth for inferred-type hints. 0 disables inference beyond declared types + literals; higher values follow function-call return types, identifier bindings, and type aliases. Alias-chain cycles are guarded by a visited set, so larger values are safe.

limits.description: Configurable resource caps. Defaults are conservative; lowering applies prospectively (no proactive eviction). The body-size cap is separate: set the HARE_LSP_MAX_BODY_BYTES environment variable before launching the server because the transport must be configured before `initialize` arrives.

maxOpenDocuments.description: Maximum number of documents the server will keep open at once.

maxTotalBufferBytes.description: Maximum total bytes summed across all open document buffers (default 256 MiB).

maxPendingRequests.description: Maximum number of in-flight server-initiated requests awaiting a response.

maxCancelledIds.description: Maximum number of cancelled-but-not-yet-cleared request ids.

maxDiagnosticsPerFile.description: Maximum diagnostics published per file (excess collapsed into a single trailing diagnostic).

maxWorkspaceIndexEntries.description: Maximum number of workspace-index entries. Whatever you configure is what the server enforces; there is no additional non-configurable ceiling.
